tldr: Anthropic expands Claude's Microsoft 365 integration to all plans including Free, giving every user read access to Outlook, Teams, OneDrive, and SharePoint inside conversations
Pinterest reveals production-scale MCP ecosystem with domain-specific servers, a central registry, and human-in-the-loop approval saving 7,000 engineering hours per month
Cursor ships Composer 2, a proprietary coding model built on Kimi K2.5 that beats Claude Opus 4.6 on coding benchmarks at $0.50/M input tokens
NVIDIA open-sources Agent Toolkit with OpenShell runtime at GTC 2026, backed by Adobe, Salesforce, SAP, and 14 other enterprise partners

top_stories.title: Claude Now Reads Your Microsoft 365 Data on Every Plan, Including Free
top_stories.summary: Anthropic expanded its Microsoft 365 connector from enterprise-only to every Claude plan, including Free. The read-only integration lets Claude search and reference Outlook emails, Teams chats, SharePoint documents, OneDrive files, and calendar events during conversations. Organizations authenticate through Microsoft Entra, and users enable it in Claude settings.
top_stories.bull_case: Context-aware AI assistants that can pull from your actual work data are a massive productivity unlock. Making this free democratizes the kind of deep-context workflows that were previously gated behind enterprise contracts. This positions Claude as the workplace AI layer, not just a chat window.
top_stories.bear_case: Read-only access to your entire Microsoft 365 environment is a significant data exposure surface. Organizations with strict data governance policies may block this at the admin level. Free-tier users sending corporate data through Claude raises compliance questions nobody has answered yet.
top_stories.the_signal: If your agency runs on Microsoft 365, this is worth testing immediately. Claude can now search your Outlook threads, reference shared documents, and pull meeting context without you manually copying anything. Set it up on a test account first to evaluate what data flows through, then pitch it to your team as a research accelerator for client briefs and internal knowledge retrieval.

top_stories.title: Pinterest Deploys Production-Scale MCP Ecosystem, Saves 7,000 Hours Monthly
top_stories.summary: Pinterest engineering built a fleet of domain-specific MCP servers for Presto, Spark, and Airflow connected through a central registry with mandatory human-in-the-loop approval. At 66,000 monthly invocations across 844 active users, the system saves roughly 7,000 hours per month. Two-layer authorization enforces access via end-user JWTs and service mesh identities, with every MCP server passing Security, Legal/Privacy, and GenAI review before production.
top_stories.bull_case: This is the first major public case study of MCP at enterprise production scale. Pinterest's architecture (domain-specific servers + central registry + human approval) is a replicable blueprint for any org with 500+ engineers. It validates MCP as the standard for AI tool integration, not just a protocol experiment.
top_stories.bear_case: Pinterest's scale and engineering resources are not representative of most organizations. The 7,000 hours saved figure lacks methodology details. Security researchers flagged MCP tool poisoning attacks the same week, suggesting the governance layer may be less mature than the architecture implies.
top_stories.the_signal: If you're building internal tools or workflows with MCP servers, study Pinterest's three-layer architecture: domain-specific servers that limit context bloat, a central registry for discovery and governance, and mandatory human approval for sensitive operations. This is the pattern that will separate production MCP deployments from hobby projects.

product_radar.name: Augment Code Intent
product_radar.description: macOS-native multi-agent orchestration workspace now in public beta. A coordinator agent breaks tasks into a living spec, fans work to parallel implementor agents in isolated git worktrees, then a verifier checks results. Works with Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode alongside Augment's native agents.

product_radar.name: Cursor Composer 2
product_radar.description: Cursor's third-generation proprietary coding model built on Kimi K2.5 with custom reinforcement learning. Scores 61.3 on CursorBench and 73.7 on SWE-bench Multilingual, beating Claude Opus 4.6 on coding benchmarks. Priced at $0.50/M input tokens, roughly one-third the cost of frontier alternatives.

product_radar.name: NVIDIA Agent Toolkit + OpenShell
product_radar.description: Open-source enterprise AI agent platform with OpenShell (policy-based security runtime), AI-Q (agent blueprint for enterprise knowledge), and Nemotron models. Adopted by Adobe, Atlassian, Salesforce, SAP, ServiceNow, and 12 more partners. Available now on build.nvidia.com.

product_radar.name: Claude Code /powerup Lessons
product_radar.description: New interactive /powerup command in Claude Code that provides animated demos and lessons without leaving the terminal. Covers skills, hooks, MCP integration, and advanced features. Part of Claude Code v2.1.90+ updates that also added cost breakdowns by model and interactive release notes.

product_radar.name: Framer 2026 AI Upgrades
product_radar.description: Framer's 2026 upgrades auto-map Figma Variants (hover, pressed, animated states) to Framer's interaction system. Designers can import Figma frames and instantly generate responsive sections, optimized content, SEO copy, CMS collections, and working navigation. Teams report 70% shorter production time versus previous workflows.

creative_intel.title: BarkleyOKRP Wins Campaign Agency of the Year Using AI to Build an Entire Radio Universe
creative_intel.summary: BarkleyOKRP used Google's Gemini, Imagen, and Veo to create 'The Fizz FM' for Slice: 40 original AI-generated songs, synthetic DJ hosts, and a month of live FM broadcasting in Los Angeles. The 700-person independent agency's standard: AI earns its place only when it makes the work better. Their win signals that the industry is rewarding AI-augmented creativity when it serves the concept, not when it replaces the team.

creative_intel.title: Kalshi's NBA Finals Commercial: $2,000 Budget, 72 Hours, AI-Generated
creative_intel.summary: Prediction market platform Kalshi produced a surreal NBA Finals broadcast commercial in under 72 hours for $2,000 using AI-generated visuals, storyboards, and character animations. The ad featured farmers, aliens, and cowboys in a dreamlike montage that generated widespread attention. This is the clearest proof point yet that AI-generated ads can punch above their weight class in traditional broadcast moments.

knowledge_shelf.title: VentureBeat: RAG Is Dead, Long Live Agentic Memory
knowledge_shelf.summary: VentureBeat's 6 data predictions argue RAG won't disappear but will be surpassed by contextual/agentic memory for adaptive AI workflows. Traditional RAG stays useful for static data retrieval, but agentic memory is critical for assistants that learn from feedback, maintain state, and adapt over time. Meanwhile, PostgreSQL at 40 years old has become the go-to database for GenAI solutions, narrowing use cases for purpose-built vector databases.

knowledge_shelf.title: MCP Security in April 2026: Tool Poisoning Attacks and Infrastructure-Level Threats
knowledge_shelf.summary: Adversa AI's April roundup documents a shift from theoretical MCP risks to active infrastructure threats. Invariant Labs published reproducible 'tool poisoning attacks' on April 1. The report catalogs stealthy resource amplification loops, authentication vulnerabilities in remote deployments, and fragile client configurations. As MCP crosses 97 million installs and becomes default agent infrastructure, security is no longer optional.

watch_list.title: 800+ Hours of Learning Claude Code in 8 Minutes
watch_list.summary: Distilled lessons from 800+ hours of Claude Code into the patterns, shortcuts, and mental models that separate fighting the tool from flowing with it. Pure signal, no fluff. Ranked as a top-5 must-watch in a review of 25 Claude Code videos.

watch_list.title: Introducing Cowork: Claude Code for the Rest of Your Work
watch_list.summary: Official Anthropic walkthrough of Cowork, extending Claude Code beyond coding into research, writing, and project management. Shows how the same agentic loop powering developer workflows applies to creative and knowledge work.

quick_hits.title: Claude Code Source Leak: 512K Lines Exposed, 8K Repos DMCA'd
quick_hits.description: Anthropic accidentally exposed 512,000 lines of Claude Code TypeScript source during a routine update on March 31. No customer data or model internals compromised. Over 8,000 GitHub repos removed via DMCA takedowns. The incident spawned clean-room open-source rewrites.

quick_hits.title: Anthropic Pricing Update: Third-Party Tools Now Pay-as-You-Go
quick_hits.description: As of April 4, Claude subscribers can no longer apply subscription limits to third-party tools like OpenClaw. Usage billed separately on consumption basis. Full refunds offered to affected subscribers unaware the usage was unsupported.

quick_hits.title: GPT-5.5 'Spud' Has Finished Pretraining, Q2 Announcement Likely
quick_hits.description: OpenAI's next frontier model codenamed Spud has completed pretraining. Public announcement expected Q2 2026. Meanwhile GPT-4o was fully retired from all plans on April 3.

quick_hits.title: Linux Foundation Launches Agentic AI Foundation with MCP as Anchor Project
quick_hits.description: The Linux Foundation formed the Agentic AI Foundation (AAIF) with Model Context Protocol, goose, and AGENTS.md as founding project contributions. Signals MCP's transition from Anthropic-led standard to vendor-neutral open governance.
